title: Add no-info and quiet options to machine init
body:
#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?
```release-note
Add --no-info and --quiet options to podman machine init, to align with flags from podman machine start
```
[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

comments.body: @Luap99 The thing that prompted me to open this PR was the fact that the options were not properly validating when using `--now`.. and when you tried to do that, the hint to start it after creation is still passed to stdout

LGTM
but would like a head nod from @Luap99

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cblecker* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign mtrmac for approval. A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
@cblecker @Luap99 @ashley-cui what should be done with this PR?
i always liked the --quiet --quite or -v -v for verbose

> i always liked the --quiet --quite or -v -v for verbose

@baude I share that preference but `podman machine start` already uses `--no-info`.
Can we hide --no-info and make --quiet an alias?

--no-info does something different than quiet  - no-info suppresses additional information about actions that the user may want to do to enhance their machine experience, while quiet suppresses that info, as well as any status information, waiting for the VM to boot etc. I'd be okay with aliasing no-info to something else, but I believe in the original issue the no-info behavior was specifically asked for.

PR needs rebase.

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
@ashley-cui @baude can you wrestle out how to proceed with this PR?

I still think --quiet and --no-info are two different things, especially that the initial request was for --no-info and not --quiet. I'd be okay with aliasing, or something like -q 0 and -q 1 where -q 1 is no-info or something like that, but i think the two behaviors have to be distinct.
Closing this PR then.  Let's first iron out what we want/need.  Then we can reopen or create a new one.
